up.units: qualitative (supports growth as sole C source)

sec.units: qualitative (produced in spent medium; also a substrate)

growth_records.cite: Andrew Quinn et al. Functional interactions between the gut microbiota and host metabolism. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-023-01572-y. [PMC11343714]
growth_records.snippet: Only three metabolites (citrate, isocitrate and malate) supported growth as sole carbon sources in this condition... Analysis of the spent media revealed that all tested metabolites, except for the glucose negative control, were depleted by
growth_records.notes: In-vitro batch growth of S. alvi in Bee9 minimal defined medium. Only relative growth-rate fold-changes and qualitative GC-MS depletion/production were reported in the main text; absolute mu (h-1) values are in Supplementary Table 3 (not in fulltext XML), so growth_rate_per_h left null and uptake/secretion rates are qualitative. All uptake/secretion entries are experimentally measured (GC-MS spent-medium) but no mmol/gDW/h values available. No arithmetic possible without figure-source numbers.

growth_records.cite: Andrew Quinn et al. Functional interactions between the gut microbiota and host metabolism. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-023-01572-y. [PMC11343714]
growth_records.snippet: the addition of 3Hmg, fumarate, succinate, gamma-aminobutyrate (GABA), kynurenine or urea to Bee9 + citrate significantly improved the maximal growth rate of S. alvi relative to Bee9 + citrate alone... In particular, 3Hmg and GABA had drama
growth_records.notes: Growth enhancement reported as RELATIVE fold-change: 3Hmg increased S. alvi maximal growth rate 3.52 +/- 0.10-fold relative to Bee9 + citrate alone (reference). Absolute mu (h-1) not given in main text (Supplementary Table 3). growth_rate_per_h left null because only a dimensionless fold-change is available; cannot derive an absolute rate without the reference mu. Acetate production tied to 3Hmg presence (Fig. 4c).

growth_records.medium: Bee9 minimal defined medium + citrate + gamma-aminobutyrate (GABA)
growth_records.cite: Andrew Quinn et al. Functional interactions between the gut microbiota and host metabolism. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-023-01572-y. [PMC11343714]
growth_records.snippet: 3Hmg and GABA had dramatic effects on growth rates, increasing them 3.52 +/- 0.10 and 2.76 +/- 0.04-fold, respectively.
growth_records.notes: GABA increased S. alvi maximal growth rate 2.76 +/- 0.04-fold relative to Bee9 + citrate alone (reference). Only relative fold-change reported; absolute mu (h-1) not in main text. growth_rate_per_h null.

growth_records.medium: In-vivo honey bee (Apis mellifera) ileum; host diet 99% [U-13C6] glucose vs naturally abundant glucose in sugar water

up.compound: host-derived carbon (from dietary [U-13C6] glucose, via host metabolites)
up.units: 13C atom% enrichment (single-cell, time-course; values in Fig. 3 / Supplementary Table 3)

growth_records.cite: Andrew Quinn et al. Functional interactions between the gut microbiota and host metabolism. Nature Microbiology (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41564-023-01572-y. [PMC11343714]
growth_records.snippet: For the NanoSIMS time-course colonization experiment, newly emerged MF bees were... provided with pure 99% [U-13C6] glucose (treatment) or naturally abundant... glucose (control)... Bees were collected at 0, 8, 24, 32, 48, 56, 72 h after in
growth_records.notes: In-vivo single-cell 13C assimilation by S. alvi measured by NanoSIMS. Only isotope-enrichment (atom% 13C) time-course is reported (raw values in Supplementary Table 3, not in fulltext); no quantitative uptake rate in mmol/gDW/h and no in-vivo growth rate stated. Captured as experimental single-cell carbon-uptake evidence; rate left null (no numbers in text to compute).
